title: Se7en Labs: Daedalus Wallet Maintenance and Improvements 2026-2027

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's device and are never transmitted to an external service, with source code auditability under Apache License 2.0." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: Opt-in telemetry shows approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the true count is asserted to be meaningfully higher; Provide independent evidence for: The proposer says any unspent labor, hardware, or audit budget will be returned to the treasury. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.41; confidence 93.3%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.030 (clamped to ±0.05): The proposal describes a plausible public-good maintenance case and gives concrete deliverables and milestones, but the deterministic assessment identifies missing budget, feasibility, alternatives, risk, and failure-mode analysis for a large treasury withdrawal. Under the doctrine's ordering, treasury stewardship and evidence quality warrant a small cautionary nudge rather than rewarding ecosystem value claims that remain only partially evidenced.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: Opt-in telemetry shows approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the true count is asserted to be meaningfully higher.
Provide independent evidence for: The proposer says any unspent labor, hardware, or audit budget will be returned to the treasury.
Provide independent evidence for: The funded work will maintain all official Daedalus releases as cryptographically signed through an IOG binary signing arrangement.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Daedalus Wallet Maintenance and Improvements 2026-2027 delivered by Se7en Labs, Inc.
Recipient: Se7en Labs, Inc., with funds administered by Intersect before monthly disbursement to Se7en Labs

Deliverables: Protocol maintenance, node and wallet backend updates, dependency updates, security patches, platform compatibility, and release engineering for Daedalus, Hard fork readiness with compatible stable releases at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork, Leios, Peras, and Nested Transactions readiness as those protocols progress, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support, plus newer hardware wallet models as they emerge, CIP-30 dApp connector implementation within Daedalus, User support across GitHub, community forums, and direct channels, including Japanese-language support and full Japanese translation, Architecture assessment covering current state, options, resource estimates, and recommendation, Independent financial audit and test hardware acquisition
Deadline/expiry: Contract year through 2026-2027; architecture assessment by Q3 2027; IOG contract stated as closing August 31, 2026

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's device and are never transmitted to an external service, with source code auditability under Apache License 2.0.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Opt-in telemetry shows approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the true count is asserted to be meaningfully higher.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Se7en Labs assumed responsibility for Daedalus under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM backend integration, Apple Silicon builds, the drt release CLI, and Nix build modernization.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,785,333 ADA, consisting of 1,666,667 ADA for labor, 33,333 ADA for test hardware, 33,333 ADA for financial audit, and a 52,000 ADA Intersect administration fee.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer says any unspent labor, hardware, or audit budget will be returned to the treasury.
missing: Independent evidence for: Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.
Independent evidence for: Opt-in telemetry shows approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the true count is asserted to be meaningfully higher.
Independent evidence for: The proposer says any unspent labor, hardware, or audit budget will be returned to the treasury.
Independent evidence for: The funded work will maintain all official Daedalus releases as cryptographically signed through an IOG binary signing arrangement.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's device and are never transmitted to an external service, with source code auditability under Apache License 2.0." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
